page: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 2 vCPUs, which correspond to 1 physical core and 2 threads. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a 77 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The AMD EPYC 7763 processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench is weak, falling into the bottom 25%, which is expected given the 2-vCPU configuration. Memory bandwidth benchmarks are generally low, with small-block read-only cached bandwidth rated as poor and large-block uncached read and write bandwidth rated as weak. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, perform at average levels.

The E2ads_v5 instance offers a qualitative cost-efficient entry point for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 makes it suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications. However, users face significant tradeoffs in compute-heavy or highly parallelized tasks, as demonstrated by poor performance in software compilation, database operations, and static web serving throughput. It is best suited for lightweight enterprise applications, development environments, and memory-optimized workloads with low parallel processing demands.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes a 77 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, but multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations are relatively weak. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance provides a qualitatively cost-effective option for memory-intensive workloads with low parallel processing requirements. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and lightweight memory-bound applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.

High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Includes 77 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage
Strong single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
Best suited for small-scale databases, caching, and memory-bound tasks
